app.title: Pi-Scope
app.tagline: Dimensional analysis with the Vaschy–Buckingham Π theorem
app.intro: Define your physical variables, assign their fundamental dimensions, and instantly obtain the dimensionless groups that govern your problem.

input.title: Variables
input.subtitle: Add custom variables or pick from the library.
input.name: Name (optional)
input.empty: No variables yet. Add some, load an example, or pick from the library.
input.duplicate: A variable with this symbol already exists.

library.title: Variable library
library.subtitle: Curated quantities grouped by physics domain.

examples.title: Worked examples
examples.subtitle: Load a real, citeable case study in one click.

results.title: Results
results.empty: Add at least two variables and run the computation to see the dimensionless groups.
results.matrixHint: Rows are the active base dimensions, columns are the variables.
results.theorem: By the Vaschy–Buckingham theorem, {{n}} variables involving {{k}} independent dimensions reduce to {{p}} dimensionless group(s).

errors.network: Cannot reach the API. Is the backend running on port 8000?

engine.preparing: Initializing the scientific engine (Python + SymPy via WebAssembly)… the first computation may take a few seconds.
